phrases.intent_en: Ask a resident for permission to use a traboule
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, est-ce que je pourrais passer par cette traboule ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-koo-zay-MWAH, es-kuh zhuh poo-REH pah-SAY par SET trah-BOOL
phrases.cultural_note: Unlike the Vieux Lyon traboules, many Croix-Rousse traboules pass through residential buildings. Ringing the interphone and asking politely is appreciated — most residents are accustomed to curious visitors.

phrases.intent_en: Ask which traboules are open to the public
phrases.text_local: Quelles traboules sont ouvertes au public ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel trah-BOOL sohn oo-VEHRT oh poo-BLEEK
phrases.cultural_note: Only a few traboules on the Croix-Rousse hill are officially open to the public. The tourist office has a list and a map.

responses.english: Ask for the map at the tourist office.
responses.phonetic: duh-mahn-DAY luh PLAHN ah loh-FEES duh too-REEZM
responses.local: Demandez le plan à l'office de tourisme.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a local to recommend the best traboule route
phrases.text_local: Quelle traboule recommandez-vous de visiter en priorité ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel trah-BOOL ruh-koh-mahn-DAY-voo duh vee-zee-TAY ahn pree-oh-ree-TAY
phrases.cultural_note: The most famous Croix-Rousse traboule runs from 9 Place Colette to 14bis Montée Saint-Sébastien. The Lyon tourist office produces a free map of all accessible traboules.

responses.english: Start at Rue des Tables Claudiennes.
responses.phonetic: koh-mahn-SAY par lah ROO day TAH-bluh kloh-DYEHN
responses.local: Commencez par la rue des Tables Claudiennes.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if a guide is needed to visit the traboules
phrases.text_local: Faut-il un guide pour visiter les traboules ?
phrases.pronunciation: foh-TEEL uhn GHEED poor vee-zee-TAY lay trah-BOOL
phrases.cultural_note: You can explore some traboules independently, but a guided tour provides context and access to otherwise closed passages.

responses.english: No, but it's better with a guide.
responses.phonetic: nohn, meh seh MYUH ah-VEK uhn GHEED
responses.local: Non, mais c'est mieux avec un guide.

phrases.intent_en: Thank a resident for letting you through
phrases.text_local: Merci beaucoup de m'avoir laissé passer, c'est très gentil.
phrases.pronunciation: mair-SEE boh-KOO duh mah-VWAR leh-SAY pah-SAY, say treh zhahn-TEE
phrases.cultural_note: Always thank residents who let you through a private traboule. This courtesy helps maintain goodwill so these historic passages remain accessible to future visitors.
